Well, it's occurred here. It's occurred in a very controlled environment with people in a room, right, with all sorts of control and stimuli. They're completely taken away from their regular environment, their regular associates. They're kept in the room for long periods of time, made tired. They're fed a lot of sugar. There's all sorts of... One of the biggest companies that used to put them in gymnasiums in order to do their preview seminars, hooked up vibrating devices to make the thing vibrate a little bit while they sat there, has a device to distract the conscious mind. So a lot of this has been sold to people in an environment where we could temporarily separate them from their conscious belief system trying to defend itself. Very hard to do that with a sales letter. Very hard to do it with a webinar. It's just, it's not the same. And that's why it was done that way, is because the mountain you're climbing is so very, very, very high. So if you go to page 62, what you think is in their minds may very well not be in their minds. What you don't think is there is there. You think because they already own a business. You think maybe because they're already making good money. You think because they're already successful. You think because they raise their hand and expressed interest in something. You give them a lot of credit for having a certain belief system that is more in common with yours than with the rank and file, mediocre majority out there walking around. When in reality they have all this stuff, not only standing in the way of what we want them to accept, not only that, not just that, it is not only standing in their way, but they are unwittingly committed to defending it. So not only is it in their way, they are fighting to preserve it against our confrontation. Again, it's why so much personal development was done in a very confrontational way. So now you all read the book of S, right? So S was the biggest personal development phenomenon maybe ever, but certainly of its era and clearly very confrontational. Why? Because that's what worked. It's the only thing that worked. Very hard to do from a distance. So a mechanical thing to do as a result of all this, and I'm going to show you an example, a mechanical thing to do when you go home is to rethink what's in their heads, the particular people that you sell to. Some things are generic, some things are unique to them based on the business they're in or their age or their other demographics or whatever, is to rethink what's in their heads, avoiding giving them the kind of credit you've been giving them probably. Make your list of what's there. Put little stars next to the stuff. You think they are compelled to defend against you. And now you have a clear representation of the difficulty of the task. So as a quick example starting on page 63, there's a section at the very start of Ben Suarez's book. How many of you went and got and read Ben's book? Good for you. So you saw this, whether you took note of it or not, another question, but you saw it. So Ben pretty much opens his book with this list of 21 myths that will keep you broke. Because he's a student enough to realize that their starting point, even though they went and bought the book and they are beginning to read the book about how to get rich, their starting point is not really ready to be told how to get rich. This stuff is in the way. And some of it you would think elementary, primitive, so you got some of the myths. I'm not going to do them all. But so you can no longer start a business. The big established companies are too powerful to compete with. There's variations. It's harder than it was. How many people do you suppose believe today it's harder to start a business from scratch and get rich today than it was 20 years ago? Do you think that's the majority opinion or the minority opinion? How many do you think it's majority? Absolutely it's the majority. And what are the empirical facts? Of course it's easier. God, most of the barriers are gone. I mean you can start a business with an 11 year old kid who knows how to build a website. That's it. Boom, you're off to the races. But the belief system, this is an old book, but the belief system is probably truer now. They think it's harder than it was. Nobody thinks whatever gold rush is, it's over. Too late to do real estate. It's too late to do the internet. It's too late to go on eBay. It's too late to do mail order, right? How many people believe that the market is saturated in their business? It's the number one question I get from new info marketers, right? It's all, I see all these ads. My niche must already be saturated. Well, let's see. If they each got 1% market share and there's 5 advertisers, that's 5%. But really it's only 1% because they're all selling to the same guys. So no, saturation's not the problem.
